DEFENDANTS MAGNATE FUND #2, LLC, JOHN SIMONSE, MAGNATE FUND #1 LLC, MAGNATE FUND #3 LLC, JWS CAPITAL MANAGEMENT, INC., LHJS INVESTMENTS, SOUTH VAN NESS ASSOCIATES LLC, 20 PARKRIDGE LLC, 55 WOODWARD LLC'S DEMURRER TO 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE FIRST, SECOND AND FOURTH CAUSES OF ACTION AND OFF CALENDAR TO THE EXTENT THE DEMURRER IS DIRECTED TO THE THIRD CAUSE OF ACTION. PLAINTIFF HAS NOT ALLEGED FACTS SHOWING THAT AN INJUSTICE WOULD RESULT IF THE SEPARATE EXISTENCE OF THE MOVING DEFENDANTS IS RESPECTED. PLAINTIFF HAS ALLEGED THAT AN INJUSTICE MIGHT RESULT IN THE FUTURE, BUT THIS INSUFFICIENT TO PIERCE THE CORPORATE VEIL. MOREOVER, PLAINTIFF SEEKS REVERSE PIERCING, WHICH IS NOT AVAILABLE IN CALIFORNIA. (POSTAL INSTANT PRESS, INC. V. KASAWA CORP. (2008) 162 CAL. APP. 4TH 1510). AMENDMENT IS MOST UNLIKELY TO CURE THESE DEFECTS. MOVING DEFENDANTS ARE NOT NAMED IN THE THIRD CAUSE OF ACTION. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; NOT REPORTED.

Plaintiff'S Motion To Set Aside Entry Of Judgment And To Permit Additional Discovery, Pursuant To Stipulation And Order
Off Calendar May-12-2015 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
George E. Akwo, of GA Law Group, Tel. 310.435.9406, for plaintiffs;
Cheryl A. Noll, of Bowles & Verna LLP, Tel. 925.935.3300, for defendants, appears via CourtCall.

Ruling: A R G U E D. The Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows:
Plaintiff'S Motion To Set Aside Entry Of Judgment And To Permit Additional Discovery is denied. Relief on the ground of "mistake, inadvertence, surprise or excusable neglect" must be shown to have been excusable under the circumstances under CCP Sec. 473(b). Mere negligence in failing to plead the case properly, or to produce sufficient evidence to oppose the motion, does not by itself warrant relief. [See Cochran v. Linn (1984) 159 CA3d 245, 251.] Here, Plaintiff's neglect is not excusable because Plaintiff was given notice on multiple occasions that judgment had been entered, however, Plaintiff and has waited months before taking any action. Order signed in open court. Judge Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk Felicia Green; Court Reporter Melanie Gheno, CSR No. 7489; Dept. 302.

MINI MINUTES: Appearances: MARTIN CUTLER, ESQ., 800.606.1508 x506, FOR PLAINTIFF JOHN CHOKATOS.
CHERYL NOLL, ESQ., 925.935.3300, FOR DEFENDANT CHETCUTI & ASSOCIATES, INC. et al.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling: PLAINTIFF JOHN CHOKATOS' Motion To Set Aside The Dismissal And Judgment, And For Leave To Allow Plaintiff To File The Fourth Amended Complaint is denied. (CCP 473(b).) Plaintiffs are not entitled to mandatory relief pursuant to CCP 473(b). "Courts have held the mandatory provision is inapplicable to . . . failure to file an amended complaint after a demurrer has been sustained with leave to amend." (Gotschall v. Daley (2002) 96 Cal.App.4th 479, 483-84.) Plaintiffs are not entitled to discretionary relief under a mistake of law; Plaintiffs' former counsel's mistake was not reasonable. Order signed in open court. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Joyce Finley #8620.

DEMURRER TO 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jul-17-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Cheryl A. Noll, Esq. of Bowles & Verna LLP, Tel. 925.935.3300, appearing for defendants and moving party.

No other appearance. The court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTs MAGNATE FUND #2, LLC, JOHN SIMONSE, MAGNATE FUND #1 LLC, MAGNATE FUND #3 LLC, JWS CAPITAL MANAGEMENT, INC., LHJS INVESTMENTS, SOUTH VAN NESS ASSOCIATES LLC, 20 PARKRIDGE LLC, 55 WOODWARD LLC's DEMURRER TO 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT is Sustained without leave to amend as to the first, second and fourth causes of action and off calendar to the extent the demurrer is directed to the third cause of action. Plaintiff has not alleged facts showing that an injustice would result if the separate existence of the moving defendants is respected. Plaintiff has alleged that an injustice might result in the future, but this insufficient to pierce the corporate veil. Moreover, plaintiff seeks reverse piercing, which is not available in California. (Postal Instant Press, Inc. v. Kasawa Corp. (2008) 162 Cal. App. 4th 1510). Amendment is most unlikely to cure these defects. Moving defendants are not named in the third cause of action. Order signed in open Court. Judge: Harold Kahn; Not reported.
